phase: Phase P2P-B Signed Audit Exception + CI Policy Gate
summary: The accepted-risk pack is operator-signed. A strict signed-exception gate (scripts/check-runtime-audit-policy.mjs, npm run test:audit-policy) replaces the bare blocking `npm audit` step in CI. The gate keys on ROOT advisory IDs: it passes only when every root advisory in the live audit is in the signed accepted set within severity ceiling, and the runtime safety defaults are intact. Any new/unaccepted root advisory, expired/unsigned pack, or armed safety flag fails the gate. npm audit itself remains run (not silenced); only the pass/fail decision is delegated to the signed policy.
acceptedRiskScope: Phase P2P production canary readiness only; does not authorize public payment or open mainnet transfer
ciChangeDescription: Each blocking `npm audit --omit=dev` step replaced with: `npm audit --omit=dev --json > reports/ci-runtime-audit.json || true` followed by `npm run test:audit-policy`. This is a signed-exception gate, NOT audit-ignore: the policy fails on any unaccepted root advisory.

rootAdvisoryModel.explanation: Exactly 2 root advisories (bigint-buffer GHSA-3gc7-fjrx-p6mg, uuid GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq). The other 14 packages are pure cascade — no independent advisory; flagged only because they transitively depend on a root. Gate keys on root IDs; cascade accepted by extension. A new real advisory introduces a new root ID and fails the gate.

gateBehaviorVerified.positiveRealAudit: PASS (16 packages, 14 pure-cascade, 2 accepted roots within ceiling)

validation.note: npm audit --omit=dev still exits 1 by design (advisories are real, not silenced). test:audit-policy is the gate that lets CI proceed under the signed exception.

blockers: Push remains gated on explicit operator instruction.
Accepted-risk approval expires 2026-06-27 — mandatory re-review before then.
